title: Linux Multi-Client Study
author: Aberdeen Group
abstract: Aberdeen Group surveyed nearly 400 Global 2000 firms (each with 5,000+ employees and $100M+ in revenues, approximately 45% US / 55% international) to assess the level of interest and acceptance of Linux in enterprise IT organizations. The study examines Linux deployment patterns, market share against Windows NT and Unix, service/support provisioning, desktop viability, and preferred Linux distributors. Available in three tiers: Complete Summary ($1,000), Major Findings ($4,000), and Full Report ($8,000).
importance_rationale: One of the earliest and largest independent multi-client enterprise surveys of Linux adoption, covering nearly 400 Global 2000 firms, at the critical inflection point when Linux was transitioning from hobbyist/academic to enterprise credibility. The study's findings directly informed enterprise IT purchasing and vendor strategy.
relevance_rationale: Linux's subsequent dominance in servers, cloud, and mobile (Android) makes this 2000 baseline study highly relevant for understanding adoption trajectories. The survey methodology and distributor preference data inform current competitive dynamics among Red Hat, SUSE, Ubuntu, and Debian-derived distributions.
prescience_rationale: The study implicitly validated Linux's enterprise trajectory — Linux now holds 44.8% of server OS market share (2024), powers 100% of TOP500 supercomputers, and runs 49% of global cloud workloads. Every trend the study documented accelerated dramatically over the following 25 years.
